author: @JamesBrown-yn7xr
text:
My father was head of chemistry in his only actual job before becoming head of chemistry at a university for 43 yrs. His job was chemist for a local quarry 60's (fullers earth) making cat litter. A contract at the time was a geniune (by modern stardards) eco prject to recycle london bus engine oil. Still good today to remove oil stains from garage floors. Just run the oil through cat littler to produce clean engine oil. 
I am ""TnT" wondering would this be an effective way to make oil burnable? compared to your geletine method. In my dad's day at an industrial scale it was. 
Now-a-days it's cheaper to spend the money on PR to make it eco friendly.

Happy to have found you this year.. new welder sitting in box in my room, inspired by your 'how to use a welder video', watching your videos back to back from oldest to newest.. really looking forward to following some of your free energy projects in 23, here's to success and happiness!
